findings.evidence: Roadmap OneParameterSemigroups/README.md:27-31 ('general C₀ first... the contraction case is the corollary, never the silent default') and :112-119 (resolvent for real `λ > ω`, bound `M/(λ-ω)`). `existsGrowthBound` is proven in-file, so the general resolvent is constructible.
findings.fix: Define `StronglyContinuousSemigroup.resolvent` for `λ > ω` (use the `existsGrowthBound` M, ω already proven), state `‖R λ‖ ≤ M/(λ-ω)`, and derive the contraction `1/λ` bound as a corollary. `resolventMapsToDomain`/`resolventRightInv` carry over verbatim.
findings.issue: `ContractionSemigroup.resolvent`, `hilleYosidaResolventBound` (`‖R λ‖ ≤ 1/λ`), `resolventMapsToDomain`, and `resolventRightInv` are all defined only on the contraction subclass with `0 < λ`. The roadmap mandates defining the resolvent on a general C₀-semigroup for real `λ > ω` and stating the bound at the general `(M, ω)` level, with the contraction case as a corollary, 'never the silent default'.

findings.evidence: Integrand bound `M·e^{(ω-λ)t}` is in `L¹(Ioi 0)` iff `λ > ω`; `existsGrowthBound` supplies `M, ω`.
findings.fix: Generalize the integrability lemma to `λ > ω` using the growth bound rather than `‖S t‖ ≤ 1`.
findings.issue: The 'Why contraction?' justification for the integrability lemma is not valid grounds to specialize: it argues a general semigroup diverges when `λ ≤ ω`, but the natural form integrates only over `λ > ω`, where `‖e^{-λt} S t x‖ ≤ M‖x‖ e^{(ω-λ)t}` is integrable.

summary: The semigroup definition and growth-bound API sit at the correct general level, but the entire resolvent API (definition, Hille–Yosida bound, maps-to-domain, right-inverse) is specialized to the contraction subclass — exactly the silent specialization the roadmap's generality bar forbids. The general `λ > ω` resolvent is feasible with the in-file `existsGrowthBound`.
